back_translation: Therefore, let people in general regard us as servant-attendants of Christ and as managers of the mysteries of God.
In this matter that is required of managers, the requirement is this: that the person be found faithful.

But for me personally, it is regarded as the smallest matter that I should be discerned-judged by you, or that I should be discerned-judged by any human court — and I do not even discern-judge myself.
Because I am not aware of any wrong thing in myself; but I am not thereby vindicated as righteous by this fact. The Lord is the One who (royally) discerns me.

Therefore, do not judge any thing before the time. Wait until the Lord comes — He who will bring out the things hidden in darkness into the light, and will (royally) reveal the purposes within the hearts of mankind. And in that time, each person will receive praise from God.

All you brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and to Apollos for the sake of you, so that you may learn from us not to go beyond what is written, and so that you will not become puffed up about one person over against another.
For who has caused you to be different from others? And what do you have which you did not receive? And if you did receive it, why do you boast as if you yourself did not receive?

You have already become satisfied-full! You have already become rich people! You have already begun reigning as kings without us! And I really do wish that you actually were reigning, so that we might reign together along with you.

Because I think that God has (royally) displayed us, the apostles, as the last group in the procession — like those who have been sentenced to receive the death penalty — because we have become a public-spectacle for the world, both for angels and for human beings.
We are foolish people for the sake of Christ, but you are sensible-prudent people in Christ. We are weak; but you are strong. You receive honor; but we are despised-and-humiliated.
Up to this present time, we are hungry, we are thirsty, we wear clothing that is old and torn, we are struck and brutally treated, we wander without having any home.
And we toil-wearily, working with our own hands. When we are reviled-and-cursed, we then bless. When we are persecuted, we then endure.
When we are slandered, we then respond with polite words. Up until this time, we have become as filthy-refuse of the world, as garbage of all people.
I have not written these things in order to make you ashamed; rather, in order to admonish you in the role of beloved children of mine.
Because even though you have ten thousand child-supervisors in Christ, you yet do not have many fathers; because in Jesus Christ, I gave birth to you through the good news.
Therefore, I beseech you all: become followers-of-the-pattern of me.

For this very reason, I sent Timothy to you. He is a beloved child of mine, and faithful in the Lord. He will remind you all about my ways in Jesus Christ — according to what I am teaching everywhere in every church.
Some people have become puffed up — as if I would not come to you.
But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord (royally) wills, and at that time I will come to look-and-know — not the words of those people who have become puffed up, but the power they have.
Because the kingdom of God does not consist in words, but it consists in power.
What do you all want? Shall I come to you with a disciplinary rod? Or with love and with a heart-disposition that is gentle and mild?
